title: Scalable brief template + accessible charts (alt-text)
context: After M0–M1 establish the pipeline with hand-crafted briefs, M2 requires a reusable template that encodes the style guide and statistical-integrity requirements as fill-in sections, so authoring a new brief does not require rediscovering all the rules. The template must also standardize accessible chart generation (deterministic, committed alt-text, accessible color/contrast) to reduce per-brief effort and ensure consistency.
objective: Create a scalable brief template that encodes the non-alarmist style guide and statistical-integrity requirements as fill-in sections, with a specification for deterministic, accessible chart generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Template encodes all required sections as labeled placeholders: indicator definition, reference period and status-flag disclosure, published uncertainty, 'what the data does not say', main findings (sourced to dataset rows), 'not advice' notice, FAO attribution + non-endorsement disclaimer, and data provenance.
Non-alarmist style-guide and statistical-integrity requirements are embedded in the template as author-guidance notes so violations are surfaced during drafting, not only at review.
Specifies deterministic chart generation: charts are produced from committed data (not hand-drawn), use accessible color/contrast, and ship with committed alt-text.
Template includes a readability self-check prompting the author to measure and record the readability score against the target.
Evidence of reduced per-brief effort: the template is applied to at least one brief (brief-016 or an update to a prior brief) and the effort comparison is recorded in the outcome ledger.
Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md — Solution approach (step 5: Author the brief)
output: A committed scalable brief template (Markdown or structured format) with accessible chart specification, ready for use by all subsequent brief-authoring tasks.
